sources.title: A systematic review of the safety of probiotics
Aged garlic extract may be safe for patients on warfarin therapy
Exploring the Cardiovascular Potential of Artichoke—A Comprehensive Review

The benefits and risks of beetroot juice consumption: a systematic review
The Effects of Oral Taurine on Resting Blood Pressure in Humans: a Meta-Analysis
The Clinical Efficacy and Safety of Tulsi in Humans: A Systematic Review of the Literature

Is there a risk of bleeding associated with standardized Ginkgo biloba extract therapy? A systematic review and meta-analysis

changes.new_value: Use only with clinician oversight in people who are critically ill, severely immunocompromised, recently postoperative, or otherwise medically fragile; rare invasive infections have been reported, and risk varies by host and strain.
changes.rationale: Replaces an unlabeled severity token with the at-risk populations identified by a systematic safety review.

changes.expected_old_value: Safe in healthy individuals; rare infections in immunocompromised.
changes.new_value: Most studied probiotics are well tolerated in generally healthy people. Rare invasive infections have been reported, with the greatest concern in critically ill, severely immunocompromised, postoperative, hospitalized, and medically fragile patients; safety is strain- and host-specific.
changes.rationale: Adds risk-population specificity and avoids a blanket safety claim.

changes.new_value: Use only with clinician oversight in people who are critically ill, severely immunocompromised, recently postoperative, or otherwise medically fragile; multistrain products cannot be assumed to share one safety profile.
changes.rationale: Replaces a severity token and preserves strain-specific uncertainty.

changes.expected_old_value: Bloating and gas possible; infection risk in severely immunocompromised or critically ill patients.
changes.new_value: Gas and bloating may occur. Rare invasive infections are a concern in critically ill, severely immunocompromised, postoperative, hospitalized, and medically fragile patients; evaluate every included strain rather than treating a multistrain blend as one uniform exposure.
changes.rationale: Improves consistency and formulation-specific uncertainty.

changes.new_value: Use only with clinician oversight in severe immunocompromise, critical illness, recent surgery, hospitalization, or other major medical fragility; invasive infection is rare but documented for probiotic organisms.
changes.rationale: Expands a machine token into readable, source-backed risk guidance.

changes.expected_old_value: Avoid or use clinician guidance in severe immunocompromise or central-line contexts.
changes.new_value: Safety depends on the exact Bifidobacterium strain and host. Rare invasive infections have been reported with probiotic organisms, with greatest concern in critically ill, severely immunocompromised, postoperative, hospitalized, and medically fragile patients.
changes.rationale: Avoids implying that all Bifidobacterium strains have identical safety evidence.

changes.new_value: Use only with clinician oversight in severe immunocompromise, critical illness, recent surgery, hospitalization, or other major medical fragility; invasive infection is rare but documented for probiotic organisms.
changes.rationale: Expands a machine token into readable, source-backed risk guidance.

changes.expected_old_value: Avoid or use clinician guidance in severe immunocompromise or central-line contexts.
changes.new_value: Safety depends on the exact Lactobacillus strain and host. Rare invasive infections have been reported with probiotic organisms, with greatest concern in critically ill, severely immunocompromised, postoperative, hospitalized, and medically fragile patients.
changes.rationale: Avoids implying that all Lactobacillus strains have identical safety evidence.

changes.new_value: Use only with clinician oversight in severe immunocompromise, critical illness, recent surgery, hospitalization, or other major medical fragility; safety cannot be generalized across Bifidobacterium strains.
changes.rationale: Replaces a severity token with population-specific caution.

changes.expected_old_value: Generally safe; caution in severely immunocompromised patients.
changes.new_value: Most studied probiotics are well tolerated in generally healthy people, but safety is strain- and host-specific. Rare invasive infections are a concern in critically ill, severely immunocompromised, postoperative, hospitalized, and medically fragile patients.
changes.rationale: Replaces a blanket safety statement with review-level qualifications.

changes.new_value: Use only with clinician oversight in severe immunocompromise, critical illness, recent surgery, hospitalization, or other major medical fragility; safety cannot be generalized across Lactobacillus strains.
changes.rationale: Replaces a severity token with population-specific caution.

changes.expected_old_value: Generally safe; caution in severely immunocompromised patients or central-line settings.
changes.new_value: Most studied probiotics are well tolerated in generally healthy people, but safety is strain- and host-specific. Rare invasive infections are a concern in critically ill, severely immunocompromised, postoperative, hospitalized, and medically fragile patients.
changes.rationale: Replaces a blanket safety statement with review-level qualifications.

changes.new_value: Do not treat anticoagulant use as an automatic contraindication. One small, closely monitored trial found no excess serious bleeding with a specific aged garlic extract plus warfarin; other formulations and unmonitored use remain insufficiently characterized and require clinician review.
changes.rationale: Corrects an overbroad class token while preserving formulation and monitoring limits.

changes.new_value: A specific aged garlic extract was relatively well tolerated in a small 12-week trial of closely monitored warfarin users, without excess serious hemorrhage. This does not establish safety for every garlic formulation, anticoagulant, dose, surgery context, pregnancy, or long-term use.
changes.rationale: Adds the key anticoagulant evidence and its limits without generalizing.

changes.new_value: Do not treat anticoagulant use as an automatic contraindication. One small, closely monitored trial found no excess serious bleeding with a specific aged garlic extract plus warfarin; other formulations and unmonitored use remain insufficiently characterized and require clinician review.
changes.rationale: Replaces a severity token with formulation-specific evidence and uncertainty.

changes.expected_old_value: GI upset and bleeding risk; caution with anticoagulants/antiplatelets.
changes.new_value: A specific aged garlic extract was relatively well tolerated in a small 12-week trial of closely monitored warfarin users, without excess serious hemorrhage. Evidence is insufficient to generalize that result to every garlic formulation, anticoagulant or antiplatelet, surgery context, pregnancy, or long-term use.
changes.rationale: Removes an unsupported categorical bleeding claim and states the actual evidence boundary.

changes.new_value: Avoid artichoke leaf extract with bile-duct obstruction or other obstructive biliary disease; avoid with known hypersensitivity to artichoke or other Asteraceae-family plants. Gallstone disease requires clinician review.
changes.rationale: Replaces a severity token with established biliary and allergy contraindications summarized in the review.

The safety assessment did not establish a disease-specific contraindication for generic supplemental betaine; the unlabeled severity token is not a contraindication and should remain intentionally blank.

changes.expected_old_value: GI upset possible; high doses may affect lipids.
changes.new_value: Human intervention data were limited and did not establish safety at the applicant's proposed 2.5 g/day novel-food use. Available studies up to 4 g/day for several months did not show platelet-count effects, but long-term, pregnancy, lactation, disease-specific, and interaction evidence remains insufficient.
changes.rationale: Replaces a broad warning with the regulatory assessment's actual limits.
No specific contraindication was established for generic betaine; the severity token is misleading and should be intentionally blank.

changes.expected_old_value: Monitor lipid context in long-term high-dose use; caution with kidney disease.
changes.new_value: Human intervention data were limited and did not establish safety at the applicant's proposed 2.5 g/day novel-food use. Available studies up to 4 g/day for several months did not show platelet-count effects, but long-term, pregnancy, lactation, kidney-disease, and interaction evidence remains insufficient.
changes.rationale: Removes an unsupported kidney caution and states the documented evidence gaps.
Trimethylglycine is betaine; the assessment did not establish a generic disease-specific contraindication, so the token should be intentionally blank.

changes.expected_old_value: GI upset; may increase LDL cholesterol
changes.new_value: Human intervention data for supplemental betaine (trimethylglycine) remain limited, and a regulatory assessment did not establish safety at the applicant's proposed 2.5 g/day novel-food use. Long-term, pregnancy, lactation, disease-specific, and interaction evidence remains insufficient.
changes.rationale: Uses the regulatory safety conclusion and avoids overstating an incompletely characterized lipid effect.
The systematic review identified limited negative-effect evidence but did not establish a categorical contraindication; the token should be intentionally blank.

changes.expected_old_value: Avoid stacking with nitrate/PDE-5 medication contexts without clinician input; may lower blood pressure.
changes.new_value: Nitrate-rich beetroot can lower blood pressure, but the evidence base on negative effects and clinically significant medication interactions is limited. Do not infer a nitrate-drug or PDE-5 interaction magnitude from blood-pressure effects alone; people with low blood pressure or relevant medication use should seek individualized review.
changes.rationale: Preserves the observed blood-pressure effect while explicitly separating it from unproven interaction magnitude.
The meta-analysis did not establish a categorical contraindication for isolated taurine; the severity token should be intentionally blank.

changes.new_value: Short human trials of isolated taurine reported no adverse effects in the analyzed dosing periods and found a blood-pressure-lowering effect. This does not establish long-term, pregnancy, lactation, kidney-disease, or medication-interaction safety, and it does not apply automatically to multi-ingredient products.
changes.rationale: Adds the relevant physiologic effect and defines the evidence boundary.
The human systematic review did not establish a categorical contraindication; pregnancy and medication tokens would exceed the direct human evidence.

changes.expected_old_value: Generally well tolerated; caution hypoglycemia, anticoagulants, and pregnancy.
changes.new_value: Short human studies reported no significant adverse events, but study quality, formulation consistency, and adverse-event reporting were limited. Pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, and clinically significant medication-interaction safety were not adequately established in the reviewed human evidence.
changes.rationale: Removes unsupported categorical interaction and pregnancy claims while preserving the review's safety limitations.
The critical review did not establish a categorical contraindication for pomegranate extract; the severity token should remain intentionally blank.

changes.expected_old_value: GI upset possible; caution with antihypertensives and drug interactions.
changes.new_value: Human pomegranate juice and extract studies were heterogeneous, with inconsistent product characterization and limited safety and interaction reporting. Small blood-pressure effects do not establish a clinically significant antihypertensive interaction; pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, and medication-specific safety remain insufficiently characterized.
changes.rationale: Removes unsupported interaction language and states the review-level limitations.
Whole standardized Ginkgo extract data cannot establish contraindications for isolated ginkgolide B; the severity token should be intentionally blank.

changes.expected_old_value: Human safety is mainly tied to Ginkgo preparations; caution with anticoagulants/antiplatelets, surgery, seizure risk, and bleeding disorders.
changes.new_value: Human hemostasis evidence is for standardized whole Ginkgo biloba leaf extracts, not isolated ginkgolide B, and did not show higher bleeding risk in the analyzed trials. Isolated-compound pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, seizure, liver, kidney, and medication-interaction safety remains insufficient.
changes.rationale: Prevents extrapolation from whole extract to an isolated constituent and removes unsupported categorical warnings.
Whole standardized Ginkgo extract data cannot establish contraindications for isolated or mixed ginkgolides; the severity token should be intentionally blank.

changes.new_value: Human hemostasis evidence is for standardized whole Ginkgo biloba leaf extracts, not isolated ginkgolides, and did not show higher bleeding risk in the analyzed trials. Isolated-compound dose-response, pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, seizure, liver, kidney, and medication-interaction safety remains insufficient.
changes.rationale: Removes an unsupported high-exposure bleeding assertion and documents the formulation gap.
Pregnancy evidence concerns oral ginger preparations, not isolated gingerol; no constituent-specific contraindication was established.

changes.expected_old_value: Well tolerated with mild GI symptoms
changes.new_value: Human safety evidence reviewed during pregnancy concerns oral ginger preparations rather than isolated gingerol. Isolated-gingerol dose-response, pregnancy, breastfeeding, bleeding, surgery, liver, kidney, and medication-interaction safety remains insufficient.
changes.rationale: Stops whole-rhizome tolerability evidence from being generalized to an isolated constituent.
Pregnancy evidence concerns oral ginger preparations, not isolated gingerols; no constituent-specific contraindication was established.

changes.expected_old_value: May contribute to ginger's bleeding, glucose, and blood pressure cautions; standardized isolated-dose safety remains limited.
changes.new_value: Human pregnancy and lactation evidence concerns oral ginger preparations rather than isolated gingerols. Constituent-specific dose-response, bleeding, blood-pressure, blood-sugar, surgery, liver, kidney, and medication-interaction safety remains insufficient and should not be inferred from whole ginger.
changes.rationale: Removes inferred constituent risks and documents the evidence boundary.
